V-M-060.purpose: Verify MTProto admin abuse alerts, operator review states, and confirmation-safe enforcement actions.

verification-levels.module-local: Alert model/service tests prove dedupe, state transitions, severity mapping, and safe payloads.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run with V-M-056 to prove high/critical abuse signals create alerts and with V-M-047/V-M-026 to prove actions are audited.
verification-levels.phase-level: Admin UI cannot close until open alert badges, alert list, review drawer, and confirmation actions are visible on mobile.

module-checks.check-1: Only high and critical abuse signals create operator alerts by default; low/medium remain visible as signals.
module-checks.check-2: Dedupe key prevents repeated scheduler runs from flooding the admin inbox.
module-checks.check-3: Acknowledge and resolve state transitions record admin id and timestamps without changing runtime access.
module-checks.check-4: Disable proxy delegates to M-047 revoke and marks the alert action outcome after confirmation.
module-checks.check-5: TTL IP block refuses untrusted or stale IP evidence and requires double confirmation.

scenarios.scenario-1: High or critical abuse signals create exactly one open admin alert per dedupe key.
scenarios.scenario-2: Repeated scanner runs update the existing alert instead of flooding the inbox.
scenarios.scenario-3: Admin can acknowledge and resolve alerts without changing runtime access.
scenarios.scenario-4: Disable proxy action requires confirmation, revokes only the selected assignment, and writes an audit-safe event.
scenarios.scenario-5: TTL IP block action requires double confirmation and refuses to run without trusted M-061 client IP evidence.
scenarios.scenario-6: Alert payloads do not contain tg links, proxy secrets, runtime tokens, private keys, or copied raw IP strings.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_admin_alerts.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_analytics_api.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/verification/V-M-060.xml docs/modules/M-060.xml docs/plans/Phase-43.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml

redaction-rules.rule-1: Alert list payloads may include assignment id, user id, user email under admin policy, signal type, metric, threshold, severity, and timestamps.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Alert payloads must not copy raw IP strings, tg links, proxy secrets, runtime tokens, or private keys.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Audit details must reference IP observation ids or hash prefixes, not decrypted raw IP strings.

acceptance.item-1: Admin alert inbox shows open high/critical abuse signals dynamically.
acceptance.item-2: Alert actions never auto-punish without explicit admin confirmation.
acceptance.item-3: Audit records contain safe identifiers and action reasons, not raw secrets or copied raw IP payloads.
